scope: Codex-only route normalization, dedupe, and canonicalization guardrails

findings: S shuttle records need context-qualified keys rather than bare route_s.
Single-letter subway routes are weakly keyed and should be a future lane.
Broad base aliases can survive on variant-specific records and should not be merge-authority keys.
Multi-route labels such as Bx40/42 should produce bundle/context keys instead of member-route keys.
Use a taxonomy of true multi-route aggregates, branch-pair records, local/limited variant bundles, and variant-specific siblings.
Aggregate records should use routes arrays and must not masquerade as a scalar member route.
Local/limited bundles should not present as pure local, limited_stop, or sbs variants.
B46/Utica, Q52/Q53, Webster/Bx41, and M34/M34A remain split-candidate or contamination hotspots.
Bx12, B44, and M60 are mostly healthy evidence accumulation with lifecycle context.
Slash-pair anchoring and cross-variant collapse are the highest-confidence deterministic risks.
Route key fixes belong in packages/db/src/identity.ts.
Local/limited and slash-combined variant guardrails belong in packages/pipeline/src/records/submissions.ts.
Bundle page/data-only policy is a separate materializer/schema lane and was not changed in this pass.

implemented_changes.summary: Added deterministic exact two-route bundle keys, context-qualified S shuttle keys, limited/local-limited route suffix handling, and pruning of generic base keys from route record identity keys.
Added route identity tests for 42 St versus Rockaway Park S shuttles, slash-paired route labels not emitting member-route keys, and large route lists not collapsing to the first two listed routes.
Extended route submission validation so Local/Limited bundle text in identity fields or source_route_type_phrase is rejected as one pure route identity.
Added regression coverage for local-limited bundle rejection.

intentionally_unresolved: Existing canonical JSONL route records were not hand-edited or split.
Single-letter subway route identity keys need a separate mode-qualified key pass.
Large aggregate route records may need route_scope or data-only materializer policy.
B46/Utica, Q52/Q53, M34/M34A, Webster/Bx41, and Bx40/42 need bounded follow-up lanes for deterministic split or bundle representation.
next_lanes: Mode-qualified identity keys for single-letter subway routes.
Route_scope/schema and materializer policy for large route aggregates and service bundles.
Deterministic split plan for B46/Utica and M34/M34A denormalization hotspots.
Review whether source-specific route aliases should be excluded from merge-authority identity keys more broadly.
